pr.title: Work so far on matching lbcommand
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Continued lbcommand decompilation by replacing several raw/inline-asm command handlers with C implementations, renaming the dispatcher from Command_80005B64 to Command_Execute, correcting the command dispatch table to sequential Command_00 through Command_09 entries, and updating assembly callsite labels in fighter, item, and lb asm files. The PR also changed CommandInfo::event_return from a single pointer to a small pointer array to model the return stack behavior, added FLT_MAX to dolphin/types.h for timer initialization, and used pragma peephole around the C dispatcher to help match codegen.
what_changed: Renamed the command dispatcher symbol from Command_80005B64 to Command_Execute and updated references in include/functions.h and asm callsites.
Updated assembly call labels in asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s, asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s, and asm/melee/lb/code_8000F9F8.s from Command_80005B64 to Command_Execute without changing branch opcodes.
Changed the lbcommand dispatch table lbl_803B9840 from a void* table with sparse/old names to a typed function-pointer array: void (*lbl_803B9840[16])(CommandInfo*).
Reordered/renamed command handlers so dispatch indices map to Command_00, Command_01, Command_02, Command_03, Command_04, Command_05, Command_06, Command_07, Command_08, and Command_09.
Converted multiple previously inline-asm or NON_MATCHING command handlers into C, including SetLoop, Subroutine, Return, GoTo, and SetTimerAnimation behavior.
Changed CommandInfo::event_return from u32* event_return to u32* event_return[4], with a comment that the array size is currently made up.
Added FLT_MAX as 3.4028235E38 to include/dolphin/types.h and used it in Command_08 for timer setup.
Replaced the hand-written asm dispatcher with a C BOOL Command_Execute(CommandInfo* info, u32 command) that checks command < 10, calls lbl_803B9840[command](info), and returns TRUE/FALSE.
Wrapped Command_Execute with #pragma push, #pragma peephole on, and #pragma pop, indicating codegen tuning for matching.
smart_moves: Typing lbl_803B9840 as an array of CommandInfo callback functions documents the dispatch table contract and avoids opaque void* function-pointer usage.
Renaming Command_80005B64 to Command_Execute made downstream asm callsites more searchable and semantically meaningful while preserving existing addresses/opcodes in asm labels.

Modeling event_return as an indexed stack/array enabled concise C expressions such as info->event_return[info->loop_count++] and info->event_return[info->loop_count -= 1], matching the observed stack-like return behavior better than a single pointer field.
Using FLT_MAX for the SetTimerAnimation command replaced a magic float constant with a named constant while retaining the exact value used by the assembly.
Keeping Command_04 and Command_09 as inline asm while converting simpler handlers to C reflects an incremental matching strategy: decompile what can match now and leave harder routines as asm.
The dispatcher C implementation preserves the original command range guard: commands below 10 dispatch through the table and return TRUE; others return FALSE.
decomp_lessons: For command interpreters, first establish the dispatch table type and index-to-command mapping; this can unlock better names and smaller C handlers across the file.
If assembly shows repeated storage at struct base plus loop_count-scaled offsets, consider that a field may be an inline array/stack rather than a single pointer.
Semantic renames should be propagated to asm callsite labels and global function comments so future decomp work sees one canonical name.

When replacing inline asm with C, preserve exact pointer arithmetic forms where they correspond to lwz/stw patterns, such as incrementing data_position by one u32 slot before reading a target pointer.
Small standard constants used in matching, such as FLT_MAX, may need to be introduced into shared headers when the project lacks libc-style definitions.
Compiler pragmas can be used locally around newly decompiled functions to recover matching codegen without affecting surrounding code.
Document uncertainty directly in headers; this PR marks event_return[4] with 'Array Size is purely made-up for now', preventing the tentative size from being mistaken for confirmed game knowledge.
naming_conventions: Dispatcher renamed from address-based Command_80005B64 to behavior-based Command_Execute.
Command handlers were normalized to command-index names Command_00 through Command_09 instead of mixed names like Command_10_ExecuteLoop, Command_10_Goto, and Command_80005AC4.
Comments describe command semantics separately from numeric names, e.g. SynchronousTimer, AsynchronousTimer, SetLoop, Execute Loop, Subroutine, Return, GoTo, and SetTimerAnimation.
The project retained the data label lbl_803B9840 for the dispatch table because no stronger semantic name was established in the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated asm callsite symbol names only, preserving the original branch instruction bytes and target offsets.
Replaced the asm dispatcher with C using a typed function pointer table and a command < 10 guard.
Used #pragma peephole on around Command_Execute, suggesting the peephole optimizer was needed for matching the dispatcher.
Left harder routines as asm, notably Command_04 and Command_09, while converting simpler stack/timer/goto handlers to C.
C implementations used post-increment on loop_count and data_position pointer arithmetic to mirror the original indexed stw/lwz sequences.
The SetTimerAnimation command uses FLT_MAX instead of an inline asm load from the 3.4028235E38 constant.
follow_up_queries: Verify the true size of CommandInfo::event_return; the PR explicitly says event_return[4] size is made up.
Look for later PRs that rename lbl_803B9840 to a semantic dispatch table name.
Check subsequent lbcommand work for C matches of Command_04 and Command_09, which remained inline asm here.
Search whether FLT_MAX later moved to a more standard shared definition or changed precision spelling.
Inspect later struct updates to CommandInfo, especially loop_count_dup and unk_x18.
